data.title: Consumer credit growth flat M/M in January
data.description:
• U.S. consumer borrowing was up $0.9B on a monthly basis in January 2025, compared with the $5.5B increase expected and the $40.8B rise in the prior month.
  • Total outstanding consumer credit stood at $4,989.8B, largely flat from $4,988.9B in December 2024, which was revised from $5,145B.
  • On a seasonally adjusted basis, consumer borrowing rose at an annual rate of 4.3%, the Federal Reserve said, with revolving credit advancing at an 8.2% annual rate and nonrevolving credit drifting up at a 3% annual rate.

data.title: Fed's Kugler expects monetary policy to stay in place 'for some time'
data.description:
Federal Reserve Governor Adriana Kuglar said Friday that said the central bank’s monetary policy will likely stay in place “for some time” due to rising risks from inflation. 

“Given the recent increase in inflation expectations and the key inflation categories that have not shown progress toward our 2% target, it could be appropriate to continue holding the policy rate at its current level for some time,” she said in prepared remarks to the Banco de Portugal’s Conference on Monetary Policy Transmission and the Labor Market in Lisbon. 

“Going forward, I will continue to closely monitor the effects of policies on the economy, and I will carefully assess incoming data, the evolving outlook, and any changes in the balance of risks,” she said.

The Federal Reserve’s rate-setting Federal Open Market Committee will hold its next meeting on March 18-19. The panel is nearly universally expected to keep the benchmark fed funds rate at between 4.25% and 4.5%, according to the CME Group’s FedWatch Tool. 

Kugler noted growing concerns over pressures on prices. 

“Notwithstanding the well-balanced labor market, there are important upside risks to inflation. Some measures of inflation expectations have risen significantly in the past couple of months,” she said.

According to Kugler, the job market in the U.S. is currently balanced and “that wages are a significant source of inflation pressure.”

“I am closely monitoring any signs of changes in the labor market so that we can keep it in the good place that it is now while bringing down inflation to our target,” she added.

data.title: Piper Sandler’s ‘actionable ideas’ – Buy NET, JD, SITM, Sell HAL
data.description:
Piper Sandler analysts presented their actionable stock recommendations, as the market engages in extreme bearish sentiment over concerns of economic growth, tariffs, and other policies.

Craig Johnson, chief market technician at Piper Sandler, noted that the investment company’s proprietary breadth indicators are in sell positions. “Our M.A.C.E. trend work (moving average cycle evaluation) reveals that more stocks are in downtrends than uptrends.”

However, he said the market’s downside will be limited, as he expects equity markets to pull in from their recent record highs toward key support levels.

“We suspect markets will find some footing as the S&P 500 (SP500) tests its 200-day moving average and the Russell 2000 (IWM) tests its 50% retracement from its October 2022 low,” he said. “A period of stabilization, reassessment, and rotation is likely at current levels.”

He added that some stabilization is due as the key long-term support is tested around the 5700 to 5800 level. The S&P 500 is currently at 5,762.

Here are Piper Sandler’s actionable ideas:

Cloudflare (NET)

The company’s shares are “retesting support from a prior breakout level” and are above their rising 30-week moving average. “Add to positions once support has been confirmed.”

JD.com (JD)

These shares are approaching the top of a bullish “cup and handle” formation and are back above their 10-week and 30-week moving average. “Add to positions on a breakout above resistance near $48.”

SiTime Corp. (SITM)

Here, shares have confirmed their support off a prior breakout level and relative strength has rebounded into positive territory. “A close above the 10-/30-week moving average should open the door to a new leg higher.”

Warner Bros Discovery (WBD)

The firm’s shares are approaching the neck of an “inverted head and shoulders” formation and are back above their 10-week and 30-week moving averages. Relative strength has risen to multi-year highs. “Add to positions on a breakout above the neckline near $12.70.”

Halliburton Co. (HAL)

Halliburton shares, however, are approaching their support off the 2022 lows and are back below their 10-week and 30-week moving averages. “Reduce positions on a violation of support.”

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Oracle earnings, crypto swings, CPI print, and more tariff salvos
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - March 10

  • Volatility watch - Gorilla Technology Group (GRRR) and MicroStrategy (MSTR) have a high level of implied volatility based on options trading. Biomea Fusion (BMEA) and Luminar Technologies (LAZR) head into the new week with a high level of short interest outstanding on them. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Donegal Group (DGICA), Intra-Cellular (ITCI), and AT&T (T). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Ibotta (IBTA) and Playtika (PLTK). Traders also expect another volatile week for crypto following the creation of the Strategic Bitcoin Reserve. The national crypto stockpile consists of bitcoin (BTC-USD), ether (ETH-USD), solana (SOL-USD), XRP (XRP-USD) and cardano (ADA-USD).
  • IPO watch - Redcloud Holdings (RCT) is expected to start trading after pricing its IPO. Post-IPO quiet periods expire on SailPoint (SAIL), Karma Holdings (KRMN), AArdvark Therapeutics (AARD), and Ten Holdings (XHLD) to free up analysts to post ratings. Klarna (KLAR) is seeking to raise at least $1 billion in an initial public offering and is anticipated to file with the SEC within a week. Investors will also be on alert for information about CoreWeave's (CRWV) IPO. The specialized cloud provider is planning to raise around $3 billion to $4 billion at a valuation of approximately $35 billion.
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Oracle (ORCL), Vail Resorts (MTN), and Greenlight Capital (GLRE).
  • All week - The energy industry will be focused on S&P Global CERAWeek in Houston, Texas. The U.S. Secretary of the Interior and Secretary of Energy will both be speakers, along with the CEOs of Occidental (OXY), Saudi Aramco (ARMCO), Shell (SHEL), BP (BP), Blackrock (BLK) and United Airlines (UAL).
  • All day - Amplitude (AMPL) will hold an Investor Day at the Nasdaq MarketSite in New York City. Presentations by Amplitude executives will include updates on the company’s strategy, product roadmap, financials, and business momentum.
  • All day - The South by Southwest media and entertainment festival will include participation from companies such as Microsoft (MSFT), Auddia (AUUD), Eli Lilly (LLY), IBM (IBM), and Rivian Automotive (RIVN).
  • All day - President Trump is scheduled to meet with the CEOs of HP (HPQ), Intel (INTC), IBM (IBM), and Qualcomm (QCOM).
  • All day - The HumanX Conference in Las Vegas will delve into AI topics. OpenAI, Snowflake (SNOW), Meta Platforms (META), and Anthropic will have speakers at the conference.
  • All day - Charter (CHTR), American Tower (AMT), Verizon (VZ), Etsy (ETSY), and Fox (FOXA) are some of the notable companies presenting at the two-day Deutsche Bank Media, Internet & Telecom Conference.
  • All day - The Emerson Electric (EMR) tender offer for Aspen Technology (AZPN) expires.
  • 11:00 a.m. The New York Fed Survey of Consumer Expectations will be released.

 

Tuesday - March 11

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS), Casey's General Stores (CASY), United Natural Foods (UNFI), and Ciena (CIEN). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Groupon (GRPN) and Kohl's (KSS) following their reports.
  • All day - Notable investor events include Eaton Corp.'s (ETN) Investor Day, Global-e's (GLBE) Investor Day, Silicon Laboratories' (SLAB) Analyst Day, and Teradyne's (TER) Analyst Day
  • All day - Albertsons (ACI) will replace Aspen Technology (AZPN) in the S&P MidCap 400 Index.
  • 8:00 a.m. The J.M. Smucker (SJM) will participate in a fireside chat at the Bank of America Consumer & Retail Conference.
  • 2:40 p.m. Camping World (CWH) will participate in a fireside chat at Bank of America Consumer & Retail Conference.

 

Wednesday - March 12

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Adobe (ADBE), American Eagle Outfitters (AEO), and Uipath (PATH) . Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Fossil (FOSL) and Cardlytics (CDLX) after their reports.
  • All day - Notable investor events include Cloudflare's (NET) Investor Day. Starbucks (SBUX) will also hold its annual meeting, with CEO Brian Niccol making some dramatic changes at the company.
  • All day - The U.S. is set to impose steel and aluminum tariffs of 25% on the European Union.
  • 8:30 a.m. The Consumer Price Index report for February will be released.

 

Thursday - March 13

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Wheaton Precious Metals (WPM), Dollar General (DG), Ulta Beauty (ULTA), and Docusign (DOCU). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Rubrik (RBRK) and Vuzix (VUZI) after their reports are released.
  • All day - Notable investor events include Chefs' Warehouse (CHEF) Investor Day, National CineMedia's (NCMI) Investor Day, Nova Ltd.'s (NVMI) Investor Day, Beacon Roofing Supply's (BECN) Investor Day, and N-able's (NABL) Investor Day.
  • 8:30 a.m The Producer Price Index report will be released by The Bureau of Labor Statistics.

 

Friday - March 14

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Li Auto (LI).
  • 10:00 a.m. The University of Michigan will release its preliminary consumer sentiment report for March.
  • All day - O-I Glass (OI) will host an Investor Day event in New York City. "The Power of Glass" event will feature presentations and a question-and-answer session with several members of the company’s global leadership team.
  • All day - Pilgrim's Pride (PPC) will hold an Investor Day at the Nasdaq MarketSite in New York. Members of senior management will be discussing with investors and analysts the company’s strategy, competitive advantages, and approach to capital allocation.

Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this story.

data.title: Appian gains after Virginia Supreme Court agrees to hear petition on Pegasystems
data.description:
Appian (NASDAQ:APPN) rose 1.6% after the Virginia Supreme Court agreed to rehear its petition seeking to reinstate a $2 billion judgment against Pegasystems (NASDAQ:PEGA) in a trade secrets case. 

The petition, which raised four different errors made by the Court of Appeals, is trying to restore the jury's verdict of "willful and malicious" trade secret misappropriation, according to a statement on Friday.

The court move comes after an appeals court overturned a $2 billion judgement against Pegasystems (NASDAQ:PEGA) in its trade secrets case with Appian (NASDAQ:APPN) in July. 

In May 2022, a jury in the Circuit Court for Fairfax County, Virginia awarded Appian (APPN) $2.036 billion in damages from Pegasystems (NASDAQ:PEGA) for trade secret misappropriation in violation of the Virginia Computer Crimes Act.

"We are encouraged that the Supreme Court of Virginia has agreed to review our case," Christopher Winters, General Counsel of Appian said in the statement. "We look forward to being heard by the highest court in Virginia and remain hopeful the Court will reinstate the jury's verdict and corresponding damages, which were in line with the scope of wrongdoing found in this case and which we believe is the correct legal outcome."

data.title: CVS Health stands tall as Walgreens crumbles amid industry challenges
data.description:
With Walgreens (NASDAQ:WBA) agreeing to a subpar M&A deal with private equity firm Sycamore Partners, CVS Health (NYSE:CVS) will soon become the only listed player to endure challenges in the pharmacy industry following Rite Aid’s (OTC:RADCQ) bankruptcy.

After commanding over $100B in market cap in 2015, Deerfield, Illinois-based drug retailer’s take-private deal worth roughly $10B indicates a dramatic fall from grace for the storied retailer in an industry fast losing its mojo.

Burdened by billions of dollars in debt, including opioid-related liabilities, Rite Aid (OTC:RADCQ) filed for bankruptcy protection in October 2023 after failing to turn a profit for consecutive years since 2019. While RADCQ emerged from bankruptcy reorganization in September as a private company, Walgreens’ (NASDAQ:WBA) woes continued.

After generating $2.9B of operating income in fiscal 2022, its U.S. Retail Pharmacy segment went on to record an operating loss of $385M in fiscal 2024. WBA’s foray into healthcare services failed to generate diversification benefits.

Operating loss at the company’s U.S. Healthcare segment jumped to $14.2B in FY24, up from $828M in FY22, as WBA swung to a net loss of $8.6B from $4.3B of net income two years ago.

Meanwhile, CVS’ (NYSE:CVS) pharmacy business fared better. Operating income generated from its Pharmacy & Consumer Wellness segment rose to $4.8B, up from $3.6B in 2022, driving the company’s net income to $4.6B, ~6% higher from two years ago.

Notably, CVS’ Health Services segment, which houses its Pharmacy Benefit Manager Caremark, has cushioned the company from weaknesses elsewhere, generating nearly ~$7.0B in operating income last year compared to ~$6.6B in 2022.

Operating in a challenging industry, the duo's share prices illustrate their contrasting narratives. While both have vastly underperformed the S&P 500 (SP500) over the past 12 months, CVS (NYSE:CVS) has only lost ~11%, compared to Walgreens' (NASDAQ:WBA) ~46% drop.

data.title: The DOJ begins investigation to determine if egg producers are slowing down flock rebuilding to keep prices elevated
data.description:
The U.S. Justice Department has opened an investigation into the cause of soaring egg prices. In particular, the DOJ is anticipated to focus on whether large producers have conspired to raise prices or limit supply. 

Egg prices have doubled over the past year, partly due to the deadliest avian flu outbreak in U.S. history, which has killed over 150 million birds. Despite the outbreak, some critics argue that producers are intentionally slowing down flock rebuilding to maintain high prices. The DOJ investigation is in its early stages and may not lead to formal action, according to The Wall Street Journal.

Last month, U.S. Secretary of Agriculture Brooke Rollins announced a $1 billion-dollar comprehensive strategy to curb highly pathogenic avian influenza. The USDA will expand its highly successful Wildlife Biosecurity Assessments to producers across the nation, beginning with egg-layer facilities, to safeguard farms from the cause of 83% of HPAI cases, which is the transmission from wild birds. The additional safety measures have proven to minimize flu cases, as the approximately 150 facilities that follow these protocols have had only one outbreak.

Shares of Cal-Maine Foods (NASDAQ:CALM) are well off their highs, but are still up more than 50% over the last 52 weeks.

data.title: Coca-Cola is said to be eyeing a new N. American ad partner instead of WPP
data.description:
Coca-Cola (NYSE:KO) is considering a new advertising partner for North America instead of its existing media account holder WPP (NYSE:WPP), Digiday reported Friday, citing three sources.

The report said Publicis Groupe (OTCQX:PUBGY) is the frontrunner to handle Coke's media account in North America, which is reportedly worth up to $700M, including Canada, according to the sources.

WPP would not be shut out completely even if the Coke media account goes to Publicis. It remains the beverage giant's global marketing partner under the OpenX model, which includes media and creative, the report said.

While Coke's media account in North America is in flux, sources told Digiday the region’s creative business is expected to stay put.

The sources also added that Publicis is "most likely offering to reduce Coca-Cola’s cost for services."

Publicis shares are up 4% at $25.94 on the OTC exchange by afternoon.

data.title: Trump Organization files suit against Capital One over 2021 account closures
data.description:
President Donald Trump's privately-owned conglomerate on Friday filed a lawsuit against Capital One (NYSE:COF), accusing the bank of wrongfully terminating more than 300 company accounts in 2021.

Capital One's (NYSE:COF) move to "debank" The Trump Organization "was a clear attack on free speech and free enterprise that flies in the face of the bedrock principles and freedoms that define our country," said Eric Trump, Donald Trump's son and executive vice president of The Trump Organization, in a post on social media platform X.

"Moreover, the arbitrary closure of these accounts, without justifiable cause, reflects a broader effort to silence and undermine the success of the Trump Organization and those who dare to express their political views."

The suit, filed in Miami-Dade County, Florida, seeks to "hold Capital One accountable for the millions of dollars in damages they caused, not just to our company, but to the many dozens of properties, hundreds of tenants and thousands of Trump Organization employees who relied on these accounts for their livelihoods," he wrote.

Capital One (COF) did not immediately respond to a request for comment by Seeking Alpha.

COF shares dipped 2.5% in midafternoon trading.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Incannex Healthcare prices $12.5M private placement
data.description:
• Incannex Healthcare (NASDAQ:IXHL) said on Friday that it has entered into security purchase agreements for the purchase and sale of 11.57M shares of the company’s common stock for a purchase price of $1.08 per share and Series A common stock warrants at an initial exercise price of $2.16 per share.
  • The offering is being priced at the market under Nasdaq rules.
  • Aggregate gross proceeds from the offering are expected to be approximately $12.5M.
  • Incannex intends to use the net proceeds from the private placement to fund ongoing clinical trials, including the completion and topline readout of the U.S. Phase 2 study of IHL-42X, and its expansion into the Phase 3 portion of the study, with sites in the U.K. and the U.S.
  • The proceeds will also be used for the repayment of outstanding convertible debentures, as working capital, and for general corporate purposes.
  • Incannex Healthcare (IXHL) shares were down more than 6% during Friday trading hours.
